text: Hello? Hello? Good morning. My name is Cassie. So I'm calling because your Medicare member profile hasn't been transferred into Medicare's national database for 2026 yet. And that can hold up your coverage approvals. So you do have both part A and part B, right? I guess. You do have both part A and part B of Medicare, right? Yes, I told you. You do qualify forwarding you to our supervisor now. Please hold. Thanks for holding on the line. This is Harvey. Okay. Leslie, now we are in the special enrollment period. That's why we are connected because Medicare has updated all the benefits. Do you have Medicaid as all of you, just some Medicare? Medicare and Medicare. All right. And God forbid, have you been diagnosed with any chronic conditions like diabetes, arthritis, COPD or heart conditions? Diabetes. I'm not sorry. I like arthritis. Diabetes and arthritis. You have rheumatoid arthritis or you just have the common arthritis. All right. So do you know how much money are you getting on your food and groceries as of now? I even get food and groceries. Unless you have diabetes and you have arthritis, you can qualify for the food card. That food card is actually a preloaded debit card. It can help you buy groceries, utilities, food allowances and over-the-counter benefits as well. Do you know the name of the insurance that you're getting, like Humana, UnitedHealthcare or anything like that? I get Kaiser. I go to Kaiser. So is it your Kaiser? Advantage or supplement? Advantage. Can you grab your Kaiser card for a moment? I just want to see the name of the plan, so we can have you in a battery. I got... Oh. Okay. Advantage. All right. Do you have your card handy, ma'am? Yes. HMO. All right. It is Kaiser. Advantage. HMO? Mm-hmm. Did you get it to your employer or did you get it on your own? Oh, when I used to work, I used to get... I used to get it to my employer. All right. So... I ain't working on it. I don't want... No. As you have retired, so do you get any medical benefits to your employer or you just got your Kaiser by yourself? I get Kaiser by myself, you know. All right. So what's going to be your zip code so I can better assist you there? 802-1-1. 802-1-1. And just for the verification, may I ask how old are you? 66. Do you still make your decisions or someone got power for turning over you? No, I make my own decisions. Well, I would say that is more good because it's actually always good to be the boss of yourself. So as you have your advantage plan, when it comes to your dental, vision, and hearing benefits, what do you think which benefit is going to be more important for you, like more dental, more vision, and more hearing benefits? Dental and glasses? Dental and vision? All right. And do you need any help with your transportation as well, like free rides to hospital? Dr. O'Connor's are going to church? Yes. All right. Tell me one more thing, by the way. When you go to see the doctor or the specialist, do you still have to pay any copays for the specialist or for your prescriptions? No. That is wonderful. And other than your insurance, other than your advantage benefits, do you get any health insurance or military as well, like VA or Triker? No. I got it. So in your six years, you are in your own home or apartment or you are in a nursing home or assisted living or public. I've got my own place. All right. And you already told me that you are not getting any medical benefits to your job. You're just on your Medicare advantage, right? Yes. All right. Perfect. Well, that seems all perfect though, and it looks like that you're a perfectly college part. And as you told me that you don't get your food costs, so you can definitely qualify for the food card now. The food card can help you buy groceries, utilities and food allowances as well, and over the counter. And your dental and vision and transportation benefits should be increased. So that's all that I just wanted to confirm from your end. Now without wasting your time, what I'm going to do, I'm going to connect you up with the best representative for your area, or we'll put you up in the Medicare system and provide you with all your benefits that you qualify. And especially as you have diabetes and arthritis. So the representative might ask you, like how many have you or what benefits are you you're looking for, actually your chronic conditions.
